notable_pick_trades: Traded own 3rd-round pick to MIA for EDGE Jaelan Phillips. Acquired 3rd-round pick from NYJ (Haason Reddick trade). Projected 3 compensatory picks (3rd, 4th, 5th) for losing Milton Williams, Josh Sweat, Mekhi Becton in 2025 FA.
roster_context: Defending 2024 Super Bowl champions went 11-6 in 2025 but lost in the Wild Card round to SF. Offense regressed significantly under OC Kevin Patullo (now replaced by Sean Mannion). Defense remained top-5 in points allowed under Vic Fangio. Key OL starters (Lane Johnson, Landon Dickerson, Cam Jurgens) all battled significant injuries and/or considered retirement. Lost legendary OL coach Jeff Stoutland. A.J. Brown trade speculation is active but not yet resolved. Dallas Goedert expected to depart in free agency, leaving TE room barren. EDGE is a critical need with Phillips, Ojulari, Uche, and Graham all hitting free agency. Secondary loses S Reed Blankenship and CB2 Adoree Jackson. Young defensive core (Jalen Carter, Jordan Davis, Quinyon Mitchell, Cooper DeJean, Jihaad Campbell, Zack Baun) is elite and under contract.

needs.explanation: Jaelan Phillips, Azeez Ojulari, Joshua Uche, and Brandon Graham are all UFAs. Only Jalyx Hunt (breakout 2025 with 6.5 sacks in final 11 games) and Nolan Smith (missed 5 games with triceps re-aggravation) are under contract. Even if Phillips is re-signed, the Eagles still need premium edge talent — this was a weakness for much of 2025 before the Phillips trade. Smith's health is a question mark after two consecutive triceps injuries.
needs.current_starter: Nolan Smith (under contract), Jalyx Hunt (under contract)
needs.depth: Antwaun Powell-Ryland (6th-round rookie from 2025). Phillips, Ojulari, Uche, Graham all UFAs. Extremely thin if Phillips leaves.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1 if top EDGE available (TJ Parker, etc.), otherwise Round 2-3

needs.explanation: Dallas Goedert (set franchise TE TD record with 11 TDs in 2025) is not expected to return per Combine reports. Grant Calcaterra and Kylen Granson are also UFAs. The Eagles have essentially zero tight ends under contract for 2026. Hurts relied heavily on the TE position. Multiple mock drafts have Eagles taking a TE in Round 1 (Kenyon Sadiq).
needs.current_starter: None under contract — Dallas Goedert (UFA, expected to depart), Grant Calcaterra (UFA), Kylen Granson (UFA)
needs.depth: E.J. Jenkins (futures contract, 10 career games). Barren room.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1-2 (Sadiq, Delp, Klare, Endries all mentioned)

needs.explanation: Lane Johnson turns 36 in May, missed 8 games in 2025 with a foot injury, and considered retirement before confirming his return. He likely has 1 season left. Fred Johnson (swing tackle) is a UFA. Behind Johnson and Jordan Mailata, the only tackles are unproven 2025 6th-rounders Myles Hinton and Cameron Williams, plus UDFA Hollin Pierce. Eagles need Johnson's eventual replacement ASAP — multiple analysts project them taking a tackle in Round 1.
needs.current_starter: Jordan Mailata (LT, elite, under contract), Lane Johnson (RT, returning for Year 14 but aging/injury-prone)
needs.depth: Myles Hinton, Cameron Williams (both 2025 6th-rounders), Hollin Pierce (practice squad), John Ojukwu (futures deal). Very thin.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1-2 (Kadlyn Proctor, Max Iheanachor mentioned frequently)

needs.explanation: Reed Blankenship (50 starts, 9 INTs over 4 years) is a UFA and the Eagles may not re-sign him. Marcus Epps is also a UFA. Drew Mukuba (2025 4th-round pick) fractured his ankle early in 2025 season. Eagles are reportedly excited about Mukuba's future, but safety is thin. Could address via free agency (Kam Curl, Kevin Byard mentioned) or mid-round draft pick.
needs.current_starter: Andrew 'Drew' Mukuba (under contract, returning from ankle fracture). Blankenship (UFA, may depart).
needs.depth: Brandon Johnson (futures deal, started Week 18). Very thin if Blankenship leaves.

needs.explanation: A.J. Brown (78-1,003-7 in 2025) is technically under contract but active trade rumors persist — Eagles seeking a 'Quinnen Williams-type' package (1st + 2nd). DeVonta Smith is locked in as WR1/WR2. Jahan Dotson (UFA) won't return. If Brown stays, WR3 is a moderate need. If Brown is traded, WR becomes Tier 1. Eagles acquired John Metchie III in 2025 but he's depth at best.
needs.current_starter: A.J. Brown (trade speculation), DeVonta Smith (locked in)
needs.depth: John Metchie III, Quez Watkins (futures), Danny Gray (futures). Thin at WR3 regardless.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 2-4 depending on Brown resolution (Jordyn Tyson if Round 1 value)

needs.explanation: All five OL starters return, but Landon Dickerson (LG) has extensive injury history and considered retirement at 27. Cam Jurgens (C) had a down year in 2025 after back surgery, flew to Colombia for stem cell treatments. Tyler Steen (RG) is adequate but on final year of rookie deal. Eagles need IOL depth and a potential Dickerson/Steen contingency plan. Jeff Stoutland's departure makes developing young OL harder.
needs.current_starter: Landon Dickerson (LG), Cam Jurgens (C), Tyler Steen (RG)
needs.depth: Trevor Keegan (5th-round 2024, backup C), Jake Majors (futures). Roseman has said Steen is the RG starter. But depth is needed.

needs.explanation: Zack Baun (top-5 LB per PFF) and Jihaad Campbell (2025 1st-round pick, started 8 games) are locked in as starters. Nakobe Dean (UFA) is expected to leave but Campbell has already taken over. Jeremiah Trotter Jr. and Smael Mondon provide solid depth. Not a pressing need.
needs.depth: Jeremiah Trotter Jr., Smael Mondon, Chance Campbell (practice squad). Solid.

needs.explanation: Quinyon Mitchell (All-Pro CB1) and Cooper DeJean (elite slot) are locked in, but the CB2 outside spot is a clear hole. Adoree' Jackson (UFA, 30 years old) started there in 2025 but was up-and-down. DeJean was susceptible to deep balls when playing outside. Kelee Ringo and Mac McWilliams are unproven internal options. Eagles are reportedly targeting the CB2 spot aggressively in free agency (Riq Woolen) and/or the draft.
needs.current_starter: Quinyon Mitchell (CB1, elite), Cooper DeJean (slot, elite). CB2 outside: vacant (Jackson UFA)
needs.depth: Kelee Ringo, Mac McWilliams, Jaire Alexander (traded for in 2025). Need proven outside CB2.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 2-3 (Chris Johnson, Keith Abney II mentioned)

needs.explanation: Jordan Davis's 3yr/$78M extension ($65M gtd) locks him in as one of the best interior DL in football through 2029. Jalen Carter (pending fifth-year option and/or extension/trade) remains elite when healthy. Moro Ojomo provides rotational depth. IDL drops from a need to a luxury — only a developmental/comp pick is warranted. The one caveat: if Carter is traded, IDL becomes a tier 2 need immediately.

free_agency_impact: Signed Jordan Davis (IDL) from Philadelphia Eagles (re-sign/extension) — $26.0M AAV
Signed Adoree' Jackson (CB) from New York Giants — $1.8M AAV
Signed Michael Carter II (CB) from Philadelphia Eagles (renegotiation/retained) — terms TBD AAV
Signed Ta'Quon Graham (IDL) from Philadelphia Eagles (practice squad re-sign) — $0.9M AAV
scheme_fit_notes: Vic Fangio's 3-4 defense demands versatile EDGE rushers who can stand up or put hand in dirt. Phillips' size (6'5, 266) was valued over smaller speed rushers. Fangio wants two-gapping DL and coverage-capable LBs (Baun excels here). On offense, new OC Sean Mannion is expected to emphasize play-action, requiring a quality blocking TE (not just a pass-catcher) and physical OL. The run-heavy identity remains — Saquon Barkley is the engine — so OL and TE blocking ability are paramount. Roseman historically drafts linemen in Round 1 (10 of 15 1st-round picks under Roseman have been OL/DL).
